channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump returning to power after unprecedented comeback, emboldened to reshape American institutions
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump, who overcame impeachments, criminal indictments and a pair of assassination attempts to win another term in the White House, will be sworn in as the 47th president on Monday, taking charge as Republicans assume unified control of Washington and set out to reshape the
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and MICHELLE PRICE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Eight years ago, stars avoided Trump's inauguration. This time it’s different
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Carrie Underwood might not be Beyoncé or Garth Brooks in the celebrity superstar ecosystem.
item.dc:creator: LINDSEY BAHR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Moving presidents in, out 'mass bedlam': Former White House usher
item.description: Emery said the process of moving presidents in and out of the White House “is almost like Cirque du Soleil.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: 5 takeaways from Trump's inauguration eve rally
item.description: President-elect Trump on Sunday rallied with supporters in Washington, D.C., on the eve of his second inauguration, taking a victory lap and teasing an expected barrage of activity on his first day in office. Here are five takeaways from Trump’s rally at Capital One Arena.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: Trump to take more than 200 executive actions on day one
item.description: EXCLUSIVE: President Trump will sign more than 200 executive actions on his first day in office—a massive, record-setting first wave of policy priorities focused on border security, energy, reducing the cost of living for American families, ending DEI programs across the federal government and more,
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump's family circle has a different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago. His youngest son, Barron, was in fifth grade back then.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE and ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden spends his final full day in office in South Carolina. It helped him become president
item.description: Joe Biden is spending his final full day in office Sunday in South Carolina, a state that holds special meaning after his commanding win in the 2020 Democratic primary there set him up to achieve his life's goal of being elected president of the United States.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: What to watch at Trump's inauguration: Big Tech CEOs, Carrie Underwood and foreign leaders
item.description: Donald Trump's inauguration as the country’s 47th president was expected to be an extravagant break from tradition — before it got moved indoors due to cold weather. There will still be well-known performers, influential billionaires as guests and foreign heads of state.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA GOMEZ LICON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Then and now: What has changed as Trump returns to the White House
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — When Donald Trump arrived in Washington in 2017 just before his first inauguration, he was a stranger to most in town. As the only president in history without prior experience in public office or the military, it was unclear how he would govern. Not this time.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Vance will make his mark as the first millennial vice president and the potential MAGA torchbearer
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — As he prepares to become the nation's first millennial vice president, JD Vance is already the presumptive heir to the “Make America Great Again” movement. Vance hasn't been assigned a specific portfolio in the White House like some of his predecessors.
item.dc:creator: JILL COLVIN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Clothes in the closets and favorite foods in the fridge: Moving day comes to the White House
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Most presidents get to move into the White House once. President-elect Donald Trump is doing it twice, and his wife, Melania, says it's a lot easier the second time around. “I know where I will be going. I know the rooms where we will be living.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden urges Americans to 'keep the faith' as he spends final full day as president in South Carolina
item.description: President Joe Biden has spent his final full day in office in South Carolina, where he urged Americans to “keep the faith in a better day to come.”.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump to be sworn in on Bible given to him by his mother, and the Lincoln Bible
item.description: On Monday, President-elect Donald Trump will use two Bibles to culminate the 60th Presidential Inauguration. He is not the first to do so, nor to use the historical Bible he chose.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump returns to Washington for celebrations ahead of inauguration
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Donald Trump returned to Washington Saturday evening to celebrate with family, supporters and political allies ahead of his second inauguration, a triumphant return for the Republican four years after he departed the city under the shadow of an attack by his supporters on the Capit
item.dc:creator: MICHELLE L. PRICE and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Marco Rubio met with UAP whistleblower prior to Trump nomination
item.description: Barber said he’s worked in both an official and unofficial capacity for the U.S. government and partners in the intelligence community.
item.dc:creator: Ross Coulthart, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he will ‘most likely’ give TikTok 90-day extension
item.description: “The 90-day extension is something that will be most likely done, because it's appropriate," Trump said in a call with NBC News.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: 1985 was the last time an inauguration was indoors. Ronald and Nancy Reagan felt they had no choice
item.description: Ronald and Nancy Reagan were disappointed. That's what White House press secretary Larry Speakes told reporters on Jan. 18, 1985, after the Republican president and first lady decided to hold his second inauguration indoors because of an unusually cold weather forecast.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: More Americans identify as Republican: Gallup poll
item.description: It is the third consecutive year the GOP has claimed the majority; a shift during the Trump era of American politics.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Trump's family circle has different look as he returns to the White House
item.description: When Donald Trump returns to the White House on Monday, his family circle will look a little different than it did when he first arrived eight years ago.
item.dc:creator: Darlene Superville, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Merrick Garland exits with his record under scrutiny and the Justice Department bracing for upheaval
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — During hearings on Merrick Garland's nomination to be President Joe Biden's attorney general, the longtime federal appeals court judge told senators in 2021 that he hoped to “turn down the volume” on the public discourse about the Justice Department and return to the days when the
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, ERIC TUCKER and COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump's first trip as president will be to Los Angeles: Sources
item.description: The first official trip of Trump's second administration will be to visit areas devastated by fires in California.
item.dc:creator: Libbey Dean, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Sen. Nicole Mitchell trial delayed until after legislative session
item.description: A judge has granted DFL Senator Nicole Mitchell’s request to delay her trial until after the Minnesota legislative session ends.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: The inauguration is moving indoors. Here's what to know about the changed plans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Sub-freezing temperatures forecast for Monday in Washington are prompting President-elect Donald Trump to take the oath of office from inside the Capitol Rotunda. Almost everyone visiting Washington won’t be able to see it in person.
item.dc:creator: MEG KINNARD and THOMAS BEAUMONT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: How could a second Trump presidency affect Minnesotans? 6 experts answer your questions
item.description: On Monday, Jan. 20, Donald Trump will be inaugurated for his second term as President of the United States. As the incoming administration prepares to carry out its agenda, we asked experts to answer your questions about what the next four years could look like here in Minnesota.
item.dc:creator: Tom Crann, Ngoc Bui, Lukas Levin, and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Tampa mayor meets with other U.S. mayors, responds to proposed conditions on disaster aid
item.description: Tampa Mayor Jane Castor was in Washington D.C. this weekend for the U.S. Conference of Mayors’ winter meeting. It's a time when hundreds of city leaders unite to discuss challenges and solutions involving affordable housing, infrastructure, immigration, and the climate.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Ohio Lt. Gov. Jon Husted expected to fill JD Vance's Senate seat
item.description: DeWine is expected to announce Friday that he is selecting Jon Husted to fill the Senate seat vacated by JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jared Gans, NewsNation

item.title: Renew your driver’s license from a browser? New Walz budget seeks online option
item.description: In his newly proposed budget, Gov. Tim Walz suggests allowing online renewal for driver’s licenses. Walz said the move would increase filing fee revenue, while making it more convenient for drivers.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: What’s next for Kamala Harris
item.description: Inauguration Day marks the first time in more than 20 years that Kamala Harris will not be in public office. “It is not my nature to go quietly into the night,” she told allies on Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Deepa Shivaram, MPR News

item.title: Biden commutes the sentences of nearly 2,500 non-violent drug offenders
item.description: Biden said he is proud of his record on clemency and will “continue to review additional commutations and pardons” before he leaves office on Monday.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Biden won't enforce TikTok ban, official says, leaving fate of app to Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden won't enforce a ban on the social media app TikTok that is set to take effect a day before he leaves office on Monday, a U.S. official said Thursday, leaving its fate in the hands of President-elect Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, JOSH BOAK, MICHELLE L. PRICE and MARY CLARE JALONICK, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Official portraits of Donald Trump, JD Vance are released
item.description: Not waiting until after his inauguration, on Thursday President-elect Trump gave the country a look at what he says are the official portraits of himself and Vice President JD Vance.
item.dc:creator: Jeremy Tanner, Fox 5

item.title: Ellison joins 13 states to defend health care coverage for DACA recipients
item.description: The effort is in response to a legal challenge led by Kansas and several other states against a Biden administration rule.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Thamer, MPR News

item.title: Minnesota courts may get final say in government's constitutional clash
item.description: An ugly opening to Minnesota’s legislative session and a campaign season that won’t end is fueling lawsuit after lawsuit.
item.dc:creator: Kirsti Marohn, MPR News

item.title: DeSantis outlines immigration plan for lawmakers to consider ahead of special session
item.description: During a stop at the Polk County Sheriff’s Office, Gov. Ron DeSantis proposed a list of statewide immigration measures ahead of a special session.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Lawmakers address red tape holding residents back from rebuilding after storms
item.description: The blame game continues on the local, state, and federal levels as officials discuss who can be held accountable for hurricane relief efforts.
item.dc:creator: Mackenzie LaPorte, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Gov. Murphy calling for cellphone ban in NJ classrooms
item.description: NEWARK, N.J. (PIX11) - Could there be a day when there will be no cellphones in New Jersey classrooms? In his State of the State address on Tuesday, Gov. Phil Murphy announced his support for legislation that would direct school districts to adopt policies that ban cellphones in K-12 classrooms.
item.dc:creator: Jim Vasil, PIX 11

item.title: NY plan would make community college free: Who qualifies
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Gov. Kathy Hochul on Tuesday announced a plan to make community college free for adults pursuing in-demand careers.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, PIX 11
